title: pulmonaria officinalis subsp. marzolae
intro: Pulmonaria officinalis subsp. marzolae, commonly known as lungwort, is a subspecies of perennial plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, due to the presence of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Pulmonaria officinalis subsp. marzolae include the potential treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in traditional medicine to soothe digestive problems and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of lungwort can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
